LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AHJAE HOLDMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST DEFENDANT AHJAE P. HOLDMAN AND PARAGRAPH 18 OF PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND AHJAE P. HOLDMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 18 OF PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANT HOLDMAN KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN OF THE DANGEROUS NATURE OF THE INTERSECTION ARE RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFFS' NEGLIGENCE CLAIM. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MOTION / NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO SET TRIAL DATE (TRANSACTION ID # 66333434) FILED BY PLAINTIFF FANG, HUAN ZHENG , INDIVIDUALLY AND SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST OF DECEDENT RUI XIA ZHEN FANG, ZHOURONG , INDIVIDUALLY HEARING SET FOR MAR-16-2021 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 206

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-16-2021 IN 206, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SET TRIAL DATE. JOSEPH M. BREALL, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS VIA COURTCALL. FREDERICK SHEINFIELD, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS VIA COURTCALL. COURT MAKES A DISCLOSURE THAT HE WENT TO SAME LAW SCHOOL WITH MR. BREALL AND WIFE WHO IS A LAWYER ALSO WORKED WITH MR. BREALL BEFORE BUT THERE'S NO SOCIALIZING INVOLVE AND THAT HE WILL BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. MR. BREALL'S SISTER IS ALSO A JUDGE IN SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT. MR. SHEINFIELD HAS NO OBJECTION. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SET A TRIAL DATE. THE JURY TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR JUN-06-2022 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. THE CASE MANAGEMENT OF 6/23/21 AT 10:30AM IN DEPT. 610 IS VACATED. ALL DISCOVERY WILL CLOSE 30 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE OF 6/6/22. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM. PARTIES WAIVED NOTICE. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Defendants City And County Of San Francisco And Ahjae P. Holdman Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and AHJAE HOLDMAN's Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Ahjae P. Holdman's motion to stay civil proceedings is granted in part. The court stays defendant Holdman's deposition and any written discovery directed to him so as to preserve his Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination in the parallel criminal proceedings. (Pacers, Inc. v. Superior Court (1984) 162 Cal.App.3d 686, 690 ["[W]hen both civil and criminal proceedings arise out of the same or related transactions, an objecting party is generally entitled to a stay of discovery in the civil action until disposition of the criminal matter."].) The stay does not extend to other matters, including discovery directed to the City and to third parties. (See id. ["Where, as here, a defendant's silence is constitutionally guaranteed, the court should weigh the parties' competing interests with a view toward accommodating the interests of both parties, if possible."]; Keating v. Office of Thrift Supervision (9th Cir. 1995) 45 F.3d 322, 325.) Plaintiffs do not establish that by providing a statement to an investigating officer at the scene, Holdman necessarily waived his Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination for all purposes. "[A] witness's failure to invoke the privilege against self-incrimination during one hearing within a proceeding does not necessarily constitute a waiver for the purpose of subsequent hearings." (People v. Williams (2008) 43 Cal.4th 584, 614-615 [by testifying during in limine hearing that his testimony in preliminary hearing was truthful, defendant did not waive his privilege against self-incrimination].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Defendants City And County Of San Francisco And Ahjae P. Holdman Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Against Defendant Ahjae P. Holdman And Paragraph 18 Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AHJAE HOLDMAN's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Against Defendant Ahjae P. Holdman And Paragraph 18 Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint.

Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Ahjae P. Holdman's motion to strike punitive damages is granted. In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements stated in Civil Code section 3294, including allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Civ. Code Sec. 3294(a).) "Malice" is defined as conduct "intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Code Sec. 3294(c)(1).) Absent an intent to injure the plaintiff, malice requires more than a "willful and conscious" disregard of the plaintiff's interests; the additional component of "despicable conduct" must be found. (College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 725.) The mere carelessness or ignorance of the defendant does not justify the imposition of punitive damages. (Pacific Gas and Electric Company v. Superior Court (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1150, 1170-76; see also Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872 ["conclusory characterization of defendant's conduct as intentional, willful and fraudulent is a patently insufficient statement of 'oppression, fraud, or malice, express or implied,' within the meaning of section 3294."].)

Defendants' motion to strike Paragraph 18 of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is denied. The allegations that Defendant Holdman knew or should have known of the dangerous nature of the intersection are relevant to Plaintiffs' negligence claim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
